channel.title: Home on Arvind Iyer
channel.description: Recent content in Home on Arvind Iyer

item.title: Research
item.description:
Current Research

Genomic Dependencies in Cancer

Cancer evolution is driven by the emergence and selection of genomic alterations. In a growing tumor, proliferating cancer cells accumulate alterations over time, some of which modify the tumor phenotype by conferring advantageous features. In evolutionary terms, these alterations are selected. Understanding which alterations are selected and in which contexts do they provide an advantage are urgent questions in cancer genomics. When two alterations influence each other likelihood of being selected, we say they are evolutionary dependent. Currently working on expanding the previous work done by (Mina et al).

item.title: Publications
item.description: For a complete list check my Google Scholar.

item.title: Talks and Courses
item.description:
• Data Visulaization with ggplot2 (2024)
    I developed a short course for doing data visulaization with ggplot2 in R. The workshop page is availabe at this (link).

  • One day workshop on data science using R (2018)

    I gave a hands on session on using PCA for perfoming dimensionalty reduction of data in R. The workshop page is availabe at this (link). The talk can be found in youtube via this (link).

  • Short Guide to dive into scRNA seq analysis (2020)

    I recorded a video tutorial for performing scRNA seq analysis as part of KeepScienceGoing Initative during the covid times for others to learn doing analysis of scRNA seq data from scratch. The video is embedded below. More details can be found at this (link).

item.title: Contact
item.description:
Email is the best way to reach me.

Details

Arvind Iyer
Ph.D in Quantitative Biology
Email: arvind.iyer@unil.ch
